name: Perfect Thin Crust
recipeInstructions:
1. Place all ingredients in your machine's fully assembled pan in the order recommended by your machine's manufacturer.
2. Place the pan in the machine.
3. Select the dough cycle and press start. You may need to add more flour (1 tbsp at a time) if the dough is too wet or more water (1 tbsp at a time) if the dough is too dry.
4. Preheat your oven to 500°F for at least 30 minutes.
5. Grease the pizza pan with olive oil or use pan spray. If using a peel and stone, dust the peel with cornmeal to prevent the pizza from sticking.
6. While the dough is rising, prepare your toppings and grate your cheese.
7. After the dough has risen once, roll it out on a lightly floured or cornmeal surface or hand stretch until a nice circle is formed. It may never be a perfect circle, but that is just part of homemade pizza.
8. Dust the dough with flour or cornmeal only as needed to prevent sticking.
9. Lay the dough on your prepared pan or peel. Pull the dough up around the edges of the pan so that a slight lip is formed, and crimp the edges.
10. For a thin crispy crust, place your toppings on the pizza and bake immediately. For a thicker, chewier crust, allow the dough to rise for 15 to 30 minutes. The longer the rise, the thicker the dough.
11. Place your toppings on: start with sauce, followed by cheese, meat, vegetables, and seasonings. Leave a 1/2 inch untopped border around the crust.
12. Place the pizza on the bottom rack of the oven or directly on the pizza stone and bake until the crust is golden brown and the cheese has melted. This should take 10 to 15 minutes.
